title: Ordivon Learning Loop
description: Formalized learning loop: finding → debt → lesson → CandidateRule → shadow → red-team → policy → gate. Every governance event feeds back into system evolution.

finding.description: A finding is recorded when an audit, checker, red-team, or verification run discovers a gap, bypass, or defect.
finding.entry_criteria: Audit, checker, RT, or verify produces a finding with severity, evidence, and affected surface.
finding.exit_criteria: Finding is triaged: resolved, degraded, or routed to debt.
finding.current_state: OPERATIONAL (QA findings, RT findings, Statement findings)
finding.example: RT-VERIFY-001: verify 6/6 PASS does not disclose known deferred gaps

debt.description: Unresolved findings become debt entries with severity, owner, due_stage, review_trigger, and close_criteria.
debt.entry_criteria: Finding cannot be immediately resolved; systemic or requires future stage.
debt.exit_criteria: Debt is closed with resolution evidence, or renewed with updated due_stage.
debt.example: CI-NOT-CONFIGURED: local-only verify, deferred with review trigger

lesson.description: When debt is resolved, the root cause and systemic fix are extracted as a lesson.
lesson.entry_criteria: Debt is closed with evidence; root cause is understood; systemic pattern identified.
lesson.exit_criteria: Lesson is recorded with lesson_type, body, tags, source.
lesson.example: L-CI-SELFCAL-002: schema-first architecture prevents dual-checker drift

candidate_rule.description: Lessons that suggest a new governance rule become CandidateRule drafts.
candidate_rule.entry_criteria: Lesson indicates a pattern that should be enforced; rule is expressible as a checkable constraint.
candidate_rule.exit_criteria: CandidateRule is drafted, reviewed, and either advanced to shadow or rejected.
candidate_rule.example: CR-001: All governed files must have registry entries (became atomic-governance gate)

shadow.description: CandidateRules run in shadow mode: evaluated but not enforced. Produces evaluation data without blocking.
shadow.entry_criteria: CandidateRule is drafted and reviewed; shadow evaluation is possible without enforcement.
shadow.exit_criteria: Sufficient shadow evaluation data collected; false-positive rate understood; enforcement decision made.
shadow.current_state: PARTIAL (shadow-evaluation-log.jsonl exists, not fully operationalized for all rules)
shadow.example: Policy Shadow Runner evaluates CandidateRule against historical data

red_team.description: CandidateRules and existing gates are tested under adversarial conditions before activation.
red_team.entry_criteria: Rule or gate is a candidate for enforcement; adversarial testing is feasible.
red_team.exit_criteria: RT scenarios executed; bypass findings recorded; hardening completed or deferred.
red_team.example: RT-VERIFY-001 tested verify honesty under adversarial claim conditions

policy.description: After shadow evaluation and red-team validation, a CandidateRule may be activated as Policy.
policy.entry_criteria: Shadow data supports enforcement; RT found no blocking bypasses; governance owner approves.
policy.exit_criteria: Policy is activated with enforcement level, review trigger, and sunset clause.
policy.current_state: NOT_ACTIVATED (Policy activation deferred per Phase 5 closure; CandidateRule≠Policy boundary maintained)
policy.example: Not yet exercised — PolicyActivation gate remains NO-GO

gate.description: Activated policies become enforced gates in the governance workflow.
gate.entry_criteria: Policy is activated with enforcement level.
gate.exit_criteria: Gate is integrated into admission, reconcile, or verify workflow.
gate.current_state: PARTIAL (gates defined in verify and reconcile; policy→gate pipeline not yet exercised)
gate.example: Atomic governance gate (check_atomic_governance.py) enforces registry completeness

loop_completeness.note: Finding→Debt→Lesson pipeline is operational. CandidateRule→Shadow→RT is partially operational. Policy activation remains deferred (Phase 5 closure). Full end-to-end loop (finding → policy → gate) has not been exercised.

lifecycle_principles.finding_is_not_debt: A finding is an observation. Debt is an accepted obligation to resolve it.
lifecycle_principles.debt_is_not_resolution: Opening a debt does not resolve the finding. Debt must have due_stage, owner, and close_criteria.
lifecycle_principles.lesson_is_not_policy: A lesson is an insight. A CandidateRule is a proposal. Policy is activated authority.
lifecycle_principles.candidate_is_not_active: CandidateRule ≠ Policy. Shadow ≠ Enforcement. RT pass ≠ Permanent safety.
lifecycle_principles.closure_is_not_learning: Closing a debt does not mean the system learned. The lesson must be extracted and the rule must be updated.
